title: sedum telephium var. chloroticum
intro: Sedum telephium var. chloroticum, also known as Golden Sedum or Yellow Stonecrop, is a perennial succulent plant in the family Crassulaceae. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Sedum telephium var. chloroticum include its ability to soothe and calm irritated skin, promote wound healing, and potentially reduce the risk of certain cancers. Sedum telephium var. chloroticum can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and salves, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and potential interactions with other medications.
